# Generated by Codex for StemStudio Script Wizard.

# Conversion Plan: PMNDRS Racing Game

## Source Target

- Source: `https://github.com/pmndrs/racing-game`
- Local inspection path: `/tmp/pmndrs-racing-game-src`
- Framework: React Three Fiber + @react-three/cannon + Drei + Zustand + Supabase
- Detected entrypoint: `src/main.tsx` -> `src/App.tsx`
- Canonical project title: `PMNDRS Racing Game`
- Title derivation: inferred from repo/package metadata `@pmndrs/racing-game` because the source repo does not expose a stronger in-game title string

## Genre Classification

- Arcade racing / time trial
- Single-scene desert track with one player vehicle, one lap flow, checkpoint split, finish screen, camera switching, boost meter, and optional leaderboard/auth UX

## Gameplay-Critical Systems

| System | Source Implementation | Planned Stem Strategy |
|--------|-----------------------|-----------------------|
| Vehicle movement | Cannon raycast vehicle with 4 wheel bodies and engine/brake forces | Custom deterministic vehicle controller behavior using source-tuned accel, steer, boost, and drag curves |
| Grounding | Cannon wheel suspension + track/terrain colliders + heightfield | Downward scene raycast against imported track meshes with lightweight slope alignment |
| Barrier collision | Physics collision against imported level geometry + bounding box reset | Forward/side collision raycasts against selected level meshes plus reset fallback |
| Camera | Source-local camera rigs: default chase, first-person, bird-eye orthographic | Custom camera states on `DefaultCamera`; bird-eye becomes a perspective top-down approximation |
| Lap flow | Start trigger, checkpoint trigger, finish trigger | Source-faithful gate volumes recreated in scene and processed by custom behavior |
| Boost | Shift key drains a finite 100-point tank | Same finite boost pool, no auto-regeneration, reset on restart |
| Audio | Engine/accelerate/brake/boost/honk/crash loops + train/water ambience | Same asset set loaded through behavior, audio unlocked on interaction |
| HUD / overlays | DOM React HUD, intro overlay, help popup, leaderboard, finish modal, color picker | UIKit HUD and overlays with local-session leaderboard instead of Supabase backend |
| Color customization | React color picker modal | UIKit preset color row + keyboard cycle fallback |
| Minimap | Offscreen top-down render target with masked sprite | Omitted as a true render-target minimap; replaced by heading/state HUD and documented |
| Train hazard | Animated train clip from track GLB plus positional audio | Runtime train motion approximation with synced ambience |

## Asset Extraction Plan

| Asset | Source | Destination |
|-------|--------|-------------|
| `track-draco.glb` | `public/models/track-draco.glb` | `models/track-draco.glb` |
| `chassis-draco.glb` | `public/models/chassis-draco.glb` | `models/chassis-draco.glb` |

| `accelerate.mp3` | `public/sounds/accelerate.mp3` | `audio/accelerate.mp3` |
| `boost.mp3` | `public/sounds/boost.mp3` | `audio/boost.mp3` |
| `honk.mp3` | `public/sounds/honk.mp3` | `audio/honk.mp3` |
| `crash.mp3` | `public/sounds/crash.mp3` | `audio/crash.mp3` |
| `train.mp3` | `public/sounds/train.mp3` | `audio/train.mp3` |
| `water.mp3` | `public/sounds/water.mp3` | `audio/water.mp3` |
| `tire-brake.mp3` | `public/sounds/tire-brake.mp3` | `audio/tire-brake.mp3` |

## Planned Built-In Reuse

- `touchControls` for mobile steering + gas/brake fallback
- StemScript scene/camera/light/fog commands for authored setup
- Imported model/audio primitives instead of ad-hoc loaders

## Planned Custom Systems

| Custom System | Purpose |
|---------------|---------|
| `prg.raceGame` | Core vehicle controller, camera states, race flow, gate logic, boost, color swaps, world animation, audio, and UIKit HUD |

## Routing / Region Strategy

- Single destination project
- Single main `.stemscript`
- Single scene, no multi-region routing

## Expected Manual Follow-Ups

1. Verify imported wheel and chassis transforms line up visually in StemStudio after import
2. Tune collision ray length and steering strength if the feel differs too much from the Cannon source
3. Check the train motion path against the imported level; the port uses a runtime approximation instead of the GLTF animation clip
4. Decide whether a real minimap render target is worth adding later
5. Reconnect online auth / Supabase leaderboard only if the final game truly needs backend parity

## Open Questions / Risk Areas

- The source uses actual vehicle physics and suspension; the port preserves input curves and camera feel first, but not exact raycast-wheel dynamics
- Bird-eye camera in the source is orthographic; Stem custom camera control will use a perspective approximation
- The source minimap is an offscreen render pipeline, which is intentionally not recreated in the initial port
- The source finish flow writes to Supabase and supports auth providers; the port keeps a local session leaderboard instead
